title: rebind pre-emptive 429 fallback to narrative — replay required
scenario: Reproduction of 2026-05-09 production incident on session ses_1f47aa711ffehMSKNf54ZCHFTF. Pre-condition: ctxRatio 0.787, lastUser msg_X with text content; rebind pre-emptive fires; codex /responses/compact returns 429; narrative wins.

title: empty-response self-heal — 5/5 hotfix path semantically preserved
scenario: Empty assistant turn at emptyRoundCount=1 with overflowSuspected=true. Pre-fix, prompt.ts:1484-1554 inline block ran. Post-fix, helper inside SessionCompaction.run fires.

title: manual /compact after clean assistant turn — no replay (no unanswered)
scenario: User invokes /compact when last assistant turn finished with finish:stop. No question pending. The /compact command itself becomes the trigger user msg.

expectedOutput.snapshotResult: { info: msg_COMPACT_CMD, parts: [...] } — treated as unanswered since /compact has no completed-finish assistant child yet
expectedOutput.comment: manual /compact behaves like any other user msg — replay ensures the /compact request itself survives compaction; the next iter answers it

title: feature flag off — pre-fix behaviour restored

expectedOutput.behavior: lastUser undefined on next iter → loop:no_user_after_compaction log fires (degraded floor; matches pre-fix)

title: idempotency — second helper call is no-op
scenario: Helper succeeds; some retry path invokes it again with the same snapshot.

input.afterFirstCall: msg_X removed, msg_Y inserted with id > msg_A.id
input.secondCall: { snapshot: {info: msg_X, ...}, anchorMessageID: msg_A } — same as first

telemetry.comment: snapshot.info.id no longer in stream → reason maps to no-unanswered, not snapshot-already-consumed; (refine in implementation)

title: race — user msg added between anchor write and helper
scenario: Concurrent stream activity adds a new user msg with id > anchor.id BEFORE helper runs. Snapshot was taken pre-anchor with the OLD msg_X.id < anchor.id, but by helper time someone else already wrote msg_Z with id > anchor.id.

input.concurrentWrite: msg_Z added with id > msg_ANCHOR.id before helper runs

expectedOutput.comment: Helper detects post-anchor stream already has a user msg; refuses to replay snapshot since it would create a duplicate

title: subagent session — helper still works
scenario: session.parentID is set. Subagent path historically did not auto-compact, but if a manual compaction happens, helper must work.

title: exception in helper — graceful degradation
scenario: SQLite Session.updateMessage throws (e.g. SQLITE_BUSY). Helper must log + emit telemetry + return without throwing.

expectedOutput.runloopBehavior: carries on; degrades to pre-fix behaviour for this iter only

title: recentEvents observed field — never 'unknown' from prod paths post-fix
scenario: Synthetic session that exercises every observed value (overflow, cache-aware, rebind, continuation-invalidated, provider-switched, stall-recovery, manual, empty-response, idle).

input.sequence: 9 compactions back-to-back across 9 iterations, each forcing a different observed value

title: INJECT_CONTINUE replacement — runtime stream check
scenario: Compare INJECT_CONTINUE behaviour before/after for two cases that previously diverged.

input.case: observed=rebind, no unanswered user msg in stream
input.preFixBehavior: INJECT_CONTINUE['rebind'] = false → no Continue injected → silent exit (intended for rebind-only-state-reset cases)
input.postFixBehavior: stream check finds no user msg post-anchor → inject Continue (NEW behaviour; if rebind also reset state cleanly this is a small backwards change)

input.case: observed=overflow, unanswered user msg X exists
input.preFixBehavior: INJECT_CONTINUE['overflow'] = true → Continue injected → user msg X buried, model responds to Continue not the question
input.postFixBehavior: helper replays msg X to msg Y after anchor → no Continue needed (helper sees user msg already exists) → model responds to actual question

expectedOutput: Both cases pass post-fix. The rebind-no-unanswered case may need a follow-up if backwards-compat is required (TBD in design review).
